rationale: The citing paper uses Ansari and Mela (2003) not only as a general example of e-customization algorithms but also to make a specific positioning claim: that prior work takes customization format/control choices as given. This contrast directly motivates the citing paper's core research question about when vendors should retain versus delegate customization control and its performance implications. Although the citing paper does not borrow the cited paper's modeling approach, the citation plays a meaningful conceptual framing role in defining the contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Ansari and Mela (2003) as prior e-customization work that optimizes offerings conditional on a given format. It refines that line by endogenizing the control/format choice and theorizing determinants and outcomes of vendor control in B2B customization settings.
